title: Continuation of the National Emergency With Respect to Trade Practices That Contribute to Large and Persistent Annual United States Goods Trade Deficits
summary: On April 2, 2025, a national emergency was declared due to trade practices contributing to large and persistent U.S. goods trade deficits. This emergency, initially declared under Executive Order 14257 and expanded by subsequent executive orders, continues to pose a threat to the national security and economy of the United States. Consequently, the President has extended this national emergency for another year, effective beyond April 2, 2026, as per the National Emergencies Act. This notice has been published in the Federal Register and communicated to Congress.
